items.question: If I do Readora Professional, am I the author of the book? Is it in my voice?
items.answer: Yes, youГўв‚¬в„ўre the sole author of your book, and itГўв‚¬в„ўs entirely in your voice. The book is created from interviews with youГўв‚¬вЂќyou are the only one contributing the ideas, knowledge, and content to the book (and with the Guided Author Program, you actually write it out as well). All we do is help you with the parts of book writing that you arenГўв‚¬в„ўt an expert at: positioning, structuring, etc.

items.question: Can I use my own writing?
items.answer:
Yes and no.

Many Authors come to us having written quite a bit of material. Regardless of what they have written, we always start at the beginning of our process and work through the positioning and outlining stages. After the structure is done, we can assess whether any of your existing writing fits into the book. If it does, great. We will happily slide it in.

We do it this way because weГўв‚¬в„ўve found that starting fresh enables Authors to prioritize exactly what they want to get from their book, what audience they need to reach, and what they have to offer that audience (this is positioning).

Often when this becomes clear, it turns out that what theyГўв‚¬в„ўve already written is not directly on point for the end result they want, and we donГўв‚¬в„ўt want preexisting material to bias decisions or lead them astray.

items.question: What if I donГўв‚¬в„ўt like my book?
items.answer:
ItГўв‚¬в„ўs fairly common for authors to be unsatisfied with their rough draftГўв‚¬вЂќalmost all Authors feel this way about their book at some point in the process.

That is why we have multiple rounds of revisions and corrections. We provide ample opportunity for feedback and rewriting. As a result, virtually all of our Authors are happy with their book by the time it gets to the final stage.

But what ifГўв‚¬вЂќafter all the edits and changesГўв‚¬вЂќyou still donГўв‚¬в„ўt like your book? This is possible. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs impossible to 100% guarantee your happiness with your book, especially given that it is your ideas and your words and your voice.

But we will do everything we can on our end to ensure that you get the book you want. If that means assigning multiple editors to work on it, weГўв‚¬в„ўll do it. If it means multiple editing passes, then weГўв‚¬в„ўll do that. Our goal is to get the best book possible out of you. Anything reasonable we can do to achieve that, we will do.

items.question: How long will my book be?
items.answer:
Book length is entirely dependent on the specific Author and the topic of their book. We donГўв‚¬в„ўt adhere to a fixed length but generally find that most books end up around 150 pages.

WeГўв‚¬в„ўve researched this extensively and found that for nonfiction books, this is the optimal lengthГўв‚¬вЂќboth for Authors and their readers. It is not an intimidating length, so readers are more likely to buy and actually read the book. For Authors providing information, this length results in a book that is substantive but not repetitive or padded. We believe that books should only be as long as they have to be to make their point.

items.question: Who will I be working with in the process?
items.answer:
Your publishing team, which will be led by your Publishing Manager. They are your main point of contact and are in charge of ensuring you get a great book.

Almost all of the writing will be done by your Readora. They are a professional writer and editor that has spent at least a decade working in books.

You will conceptualize your book cover with the help of our Creative Director, who has done dozens of bestselling book covers. It will be designed by her or one of her team, and you will talk to our Art Director for feedback and revisions.

Your marketing efforts will be led by our book launch and PR team to ensure your book reaches its audience and achieves your goals as a newly published Author.

items.question: How do you select my Readora? Can I choose them?
items.answer:
We understand the importance of the Readora-Author relationship, and we do everything we can to ensure a great fit.

To begin with, we thoroughly vet and test all our Readoras. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs easier to get into Harvard (6% acceptance rate) than it is to work with us as a Readora (<1% acceptance rate). Not only that, but our Readoras have spent at least a decade (usually longer) in writing and publishing, and many of them are highly decoratedГўв‚¬вЂќPulitzer Prize winners, Emmy nominees and winners, and National Magazine Award winners.

To assign Readoras, we use a matchmaking system to pair up the right Readora and Author. HereГўв‚¬в„ўs how it works: We get to know you, your book, and how you work. Then we create a Гўв‚¬Е“Project ScopeГўв‚¬Вќ for your book and share it with all of our Readoras. Those who would like to work on your book make their interest known, and then we arrange up to three one-on-one matchmaking calls between you and the potential Readora. Then you get to select the one who seems like the best fit.

Even with this system, there are rare cases in which the Author and Readora end up not being a good fit. When this happens, weГўв‚¬в„ўre happy to find a replacement Readora.

items.question: Can my book have more than one author? Do you support Co-Authors?
items.answer: Of course! We work on multi-author books all the time. Our co-author fee is $3,500 per author.

items.question: WhatГўв‚¬в„ўs the difference between the $44k (Guided Author) and $56k (Readora Professional) options?
items.answer: The main difference is how much time it takes you, and who actually writes out the content of the book. In the $44k option (which we call Guided Author), youГўв‚¬в„ўll use our full process, but you will do the writing and typing yourself (with our guidance and support the entire way, described here). This option usually requires you to spend about an hour of writing a day for 6+ months. In the $56k option (which we call Readora Professional), all you have to do is get on the phone, talk about what you already know, and then give feedback to revise the manuscript once it is written. We handle the typing and writing, but the words, ideas, and voice are still entirely yours. This option requires about two hours a week on the phone.

items.question: How do I decide between the $44k (Guided Author) and $56k (Readora Professional) options?
items.answer:
There are two main questions to ask yourself:
  1. Do you like sitting down and writing? Do you like typing and having your hands on the keyboard?
  2. Do you have enough time to write this yourself?
If the answer to both questions is yesГўв‚¬вЂќyou like writing and you have timeГўв‚¬вЂќthen Guided Author is probably the right option. If the answer to both questions is noГўв‚¬вЂќyou donГўв‚¬в„ўt like writing and you donГўв‚¬в„ўt have timeГўв‚¬вЂќthen Readora Professional is probably the right option. The difficulty comes if the answer to #1 is yes (you do like writing) but the answer to #2 is no (you donГўв‚¬в„ўt have time). Then youГўв‚¬в„ўll have to decide what gives: do you make time for writing, or do you value your time too much, and want us to do the typing for you? Generally speaking, assume that Guided Author requires one to two hours a day for a year (yes, you can do it faster) and that Readora Professional requires two hours a week for nine months.

items.question: I saw you had a $135k option (Readora Elite). What is that?
items.answer:
Readora Elite Ghostwriting isn't for everyone. It's intended for authors who require an authentic ghostwriting experience that often involves interviewing outside sources, conducting background research, and creating a bespoke process to fit their needs.

Almost all authors are better off with Guided Author ($44k) or Readora Professional ($56k).

items.question: Is Compass refundable?
items.answer: No, but the value is delivered in fullГўв‚¬вЂќyour Book Roadmap and North Star are strategic assets you own.

items.question: How long should it take to write a book?
items.answer: It depends. Some people finish their first draft in a few months, while others take a year or more.

items.question: How long does the Signature process take?
items.answer: Most Signature books follow the same timeline as Readora ProfessionalГўв‚¬вЂќtypically 12 to 18 monthsГўв‚¬вЂќbut the additional collaboration time means the process can expand based on your bookГўв‚¬в„ўs needs and creative goals.

items.question: How long does it take?
items.answer: Most Compass clients finish in 4Гўв‚¬вЂњ6 weeks. Projects extending past 4 months incur a $500 monthly continuation fee.

items.question: Do I own all the rights to my book?
items.answer: Yes. You own the copyright and all print and reproduction rights. You can do anything you want with your book.

items.question: Do I get all the profits from my book?
items.answer: Yes. We take absolutely no money from the sale of your book, and all accounts are set up in your name and under your ownership.

items.question: Who is the publisher?
items.answer: We have two publishing imprints at Readora: Lioncrest Publishing and Houndstooth Press. Both are wholly owned imprints of Readora Media. You donГўв‚¬в„ўt have to use either as your publisher. We can essentially create a new publishing company for you with any name you like. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs completely up to you who the named publisher of the book is.

items.question: How do royalties work?
items.answer:
You own 100% of the royalties for your book.

Although Readora doesnГўв‚¬в„ўt take any share of the bookГўв‚¬в„ўs profits, there are some costs that donГўв‚¬в„ўt go directly in your pocketГўв‚¬вЂќnamely, AmazonГўв‚¬в„ўs fees and printing costs.

For eBooks, Amazon takes 30% of your royalties and you take the other 70%. For paperbacks, Amazon takes 40% of the royalties, the printer gets approximately $3 to print the book, and the rest is yours. If you do hardcover, weГўв‚¬в„ўll explain it all before you make any decisions.

items.question: Why don't you take royalties? Doesn't that align our incentives?
items.answer:
No, it actually does the opposite. The best strategy to get a great book for you is to use the service-based model, where we are paid a fee and you own all rights and all royalties.   

This is because under a service-based model we get paid for ensuring that your book serves your goals (such as getting speaking gigs or finding clients or creating influence), as opposed to a royalty-based model, where we are incentivized to focus only on selling copies (regardless of whether it serves your goals).

items.question: Is Readora self-publishing?
items.answer:
Yes, but possibly not in the sense that you are thinking about. This is a long explanation because the book publishing business is so weird.

When people ask, Гўв‚¬Е“Is this self-publishing?Гўв‚¬Вќ what they tend to mean is, Гўв‚¬Е“Will this be unprofessional?Гўв‚¬Вќ To that, the answer is obviously: Гўв‚¬Е“No, itГўв‚¬в„ўs professional.Гўв‚¬Вќ YouГўв‚¬в„ўre hiring us for that exact reasonГўв‚¬вЂќto ensure that your book not only gets done, but is done professionally.

Furthermore, your book is published by an established publishing company. So the answer to the self-publishing question is: Гўв‚¬Е“No, itГўв‚¬в„ўs not self-publishing.Гўв‚¬Вќ

But in fairness, this question used to be more relevant. Before self-publishing existed in its current form, there were only two options: traditional publishing, or what were called Гўв‚¬Е“vanity presses.Гўв‚¬Вќ With these options, having a Гўв‚¬Е“traditionally publishedГўв‚¬Вќ bookГўв‚¬вЂќwhich meant a New York publisher gave you an advanceГўв‚¬вЂќwas a signal that you were serious and credible, whereas being published by other means (a vanity press) was a signal that you werenГўв‚¬в„ўt credible.

This is just no longer the case. Today, most books are published outside of the traditional publishing system. Vanity presses have for the most part disappeared, and they have been replaced with a number of what are called Гўв‚¬Е“hybridГўв‚¬Вќ publishing companies. These vary widely in the quality of the books they produce, but many are absolutely credible and professional.

items.question: Can you get my books into a bookstore?
items.answer:
Yes and no. This is a complicated answer, again because the publishing business is so weird.

Yes, we make your book available for order and sale in bookstores and retail outlets. All our books are put into the Ingram database, and can be ordered in every bookstore in the country.

No, we do not automatically put your book in bookstores. This is because bookstores carry very few books, reserving their limited space for established authors or books from publishers who are paying for shelf space.

You generally need to sell at least 25,000 copies before bookstores become interested in carrying your book. ThatГўв‚¬в„ўs the bad news. The good news is that being in bookstores doesnГўв‚¬в„ўt really matter anymore. A book sitting on a shelf doesnГўв‚¬в„ўt sell in most cases. In terms of buyer behavior, most people who shop at Barnes & Noble also shop on Amazon, so if they donГўв‚¬в„ўt see a book at Barnes & Noble, theyГўв‚¬в„ўll just go to Amazon. In other words, lack of bookstore distribution in and of itself doesnГўв‚¬в„ўt necessarily lose you very many sales.

The reality is, for almost all authors, being in a bookstore is purely about ego, and not about actual ROI. We donГўв‚¬в„ўt deny that going into a bookstore and seeing your book on the shelf is nice, but having a book in a bookstore does almost nothing in terms of sales or awareness. It is only a status object.

items.question: How much do you charge for copies? Will I be able to order personal copies at a discounted price?
items.answer:
We donГўв‚¬в„ўt charge you anything for copies. You get to buy copies at the print cost. For print on demand paperback, Kindle Direct Publishing and IngramSpark not only prints books for approximately $4 a copy when theyГўв‚¬в„ўre ordered through Amazon, but theyГўв‚¬в„ўll sell the author these books at the same cost. At any time after the book is published, you can order copies in any quantities youГўв‚¬в„ўd like at this discounted price, direct from the printer (we donГўв‚¬в„ўt make money on copies).

If you are looking for a larger quantity of books (1,000 or more), we set you up with a commercial offset printer to handle this request. They can provide better prices at large quantities. We take no markup.

items.question: Who designs the book covers? Do I get to give feedback on that?
items.answer:
Your book cover and manuscript interior layout will be designed by world class designers, but you have full creative control.

Our Creative Director has done dozens of New York Times Best Sellers, as well as the other book cover designers weГўв‚¬в„ўve worked with. Combined, they have worked on over 100 best sellers that have sold millions of copies. You are getting the very best in design, because having a great book cover is a key to establishing your book as credible and professional.

Before we start the design process, youГўв‚¬в„ўll have a long conversation with the Creative Director to understand your vision for the cover (if you have any). We organize this information into a design brief and use it to create a series of initial cover designs, which you will review and give feedback on. Just like all parts of our process, you will have full, 100% approval of your cover.

items.question: What marketing comes with the Readora Publishing package?
items.answer:
We will manage the logistics to run an Amazon Bestseller Campaign that focuses on maximizing early sales, reviews, and rankings, all to build the foundation of early credibility for your bookГўв‚¬в„ўs long term success.

We will also support you in promoting the book to your connections by creating social media graphics for the book, crafting a book announcement email, gifting free copies to your friends and family, and creating a strategy for you to get the support of influencers who you know.

By the end of the first week of your book release, your book should have Amazon bestseller status, a burst of early sales, a baseline of reviews, awareness among your network, and you should have a plan for how to continue to leverage it going forward.

We do offer other add-on marketing packages as well.

items.question: I have a traditional publishing deal. Can I use Readora to write the manuscript only?
items.answer: Yes. We have done this many times, including recently for Tiffany HaddishГўв‚¬в„ўs New York Times Bestseller, The Last Black Unicorn, and Joey ColemanГўв‚¬в„ўs Wall Street Journal Bestseller, Never Lose a Customer Again.

items.question: What is a Vomit Draft?
items.answer: A Vomit Draft is the first draft of your manuscript. We call it a Vomit Draft because nobody cares what they look like when they're vomiting Гўв‚¬вЂќ they just need to get it out. You should have the same approach with your first draft. Don't worry about what it looks like, just get it out.

items.question: Should I edit as I write?
items.answer: No. We recommend writing your first draftГўв‚¬вЂќwhat we call a Vomit DraftГўв‚¬вЂќall the way through without editing. Then, you will go back and edit. This will feel hard, but trust us, it works.

items.question: IГўв‚¬в„ўm terrified my first draft will be garbage. How do I manage this?
items.answer: That's okay, it's expected that your first draft isn't 'great.' In fact, we like to call it a Vomit Draft because it's just about 'getting it out' and not how 'good' it looks. You'll go back and edit it later.

items.question: What kinds of results can I expect for my book launch?
items.answer: Talking about results can be tricky because results mean different things to different people. For example, in 2018, we did a book about marketing for lawyers. It was pretty niche, and only sold a few hundred copies during the book launch. But because the author ran a large legal marketing company and did a lot with the attention we generated, he attributes over $2mm directly to the Book Promotion Process. What we can guarantee is that you'll receive custom emails for your email drip campaign, social media assets to effectively talk about your book online, a Goodreads Giveaway campaign including email addresses of those who enter, an enhanced Amazon listing, guaranteed exposure that caters to your genre, access to an exclusive international rights marketplace, online advertising to promote the book, and exposure to Readora's subscribers and followers.

items.question: Should I be focusing on pre-sales?
items.answer: No! A lot of authors obsess over pre-sales, and most of them do it only because they see other authors do it. The only people who this makes sense for are those who are trying to hit the NYT Bestseller list, or are trying to impress their publisher so they are taken more seriously. If you are most concerned with the long term success of the book, it's much better to focus that attention after launch. As an example, when was the last time you pre-ordered a book? Probably not recently, if ever, right? Instead of making an "ask" before launch, losing people, and converting poorly, we'd rather use that time to get people excited and save the ask until after launch when potential readers are more likely to pull the trigger.

items.question: How do you choose categories?
items.answer:
Categories are an important part of hitting Amazon bestseller. Basically, if you are selling more books than anyone else in your category over a 1-2 day period, you'll become the #1 book in the category.

To ensure this happens, we wait until the week before launch (when we have a good sense of the competition) and we research all the relevant categories around your topic. We want to make sure they are prominent categories, but you aren't going head to head with Malcolm Gladwell or anyone who will be impossible to beat.

Once we find the right categories, we add you to them in advance of launch week to maximize your chance of hitting bestseller status during launch week.

items.question: Do I need to do the $0.99 price promotion?
items.answer:
You don't need to do anything you don't want to do! But consider this...

The long-term success of a book comes from word of mouth, from readers talking about it. So our goal early on in book marketing is to get as many people as possible to read the book, so they can talk about it later.

The $0.99 promotion is powerful because all the people who would regularly be on the fence, or maybe add the book to their "read later" list, decide to take the plunge right away. That gets you more readers early on, and maximizes your chances of bestseller status.

We recommend doing it for all authors, unless book sale revenue is a big factor for them AND they have a big audience that they want to earn that revenue from. Otherwise, the $0.99 discount is a no-brainer to support the book's long-term success.

items.question: How much time should I expect to spend on this?
items.answer:
This varies by author, because a lot of the advice we give leaves a lot of room to do as much or as little as needed. Most of what we will ask from you has to do with focusing on your relationships and galvanizing them to take action to promote the book. Some authors don't do this at all, and some dedicate all their time to it leading up to launch. As estimates for a normal author, you can expect to spend about:
  • 60-90 minutes per week leading up to launch
  • 60-90 minutes per day during launch week
  • 30-60 minutes per week after launch

items.question: What kind of media exposure should I expect during the book promotion?
items.answer:
Like anything that we donГўв‚¬в„ўt have complete control over, we canГўв‚¬в„ўt make guarantees around media.

We can guarantee that we'll pitch your book to outlets that give you the best chance at landing an interview. This means that our team evaluates the content of your book and the industry you're in to ensure we're pitching you to our media connections looking to feature authors like you. 

This means that your book about running a business will be pitched to media interested in business. If your content could apply to parents with the right spin, that outreach is better left to a dedicated Publicist (who we're happy to recommend).

items.question: How do you go about figuring out media strategy?
items.answer: We start with creating a customized strategic plan - the Footprint Strategy. We start by identifying people who have successfully reached and impacted the audience we want to reach, and use them as our guides. We then methodically research what media the top 5-10 people in your field have secured that have been most impactful in elevating them to that level. We now have a powerful list of outlets that could make a big impact for you and your book. We also leverage our own relationships. We have over 3,000 journalists we've built connections with over the course of doing hundreds of launches, so we comb through those outlets that align with the direction from the original research. Finally, weГўв‚¬в„ўre a team of deeply experienced PR professionals, so sometimes it's not so methodical and comes down to instinct.

items.question: Can you help me with reaching out to ______?
items.answer: For the first 4 months weГўв‚¬в„ўll work on building credibility and authority with the media, presenting you opportunities at all levels. After 4 months we can discuss reaching out to individual journalists and publications on your behalf as this allows us to build out your story and reputation in the media so you are pre-vetted ahead of going after specific publications.

items.question: How does the Amazon Bestseller Campaign work?
items.answer:
Let me be straightforward with you: getting on the Amazon Bestseller list isnГўв‚¬в„ўt that big a deal.

Amazon divides their list into hundreds of categories and uses a relatively short time window for rankings, so with the right strategy, hitting #1 in your category is very easy.

However, this extra form of credibility does help as you use your book to secure media, book speeches, and share your book with potential clients.

To that end, we ensure that the marketing we do aligns into an Amazon Bestseller Campaign.

We discount the ebook to $0.99 for the first week, and concentrate our marketing effortsГўв‚¬вЂќthe email to your connections, the release of a podcast episode, the publication of an excerpt, an email blast to tens of thousands of people, and much moreГўв‚¬вЂќinto that week, to maximize the short term sales and overall ranking.

An important note: These activities arenГўв‚¬в„ўt aimed only at hitting Amazon bestseller status. They are the best decisions for the success of your book, and hitting Amazon bestseller is a byproduct.

items.question: How do I allow someone else to order paperbacks at a discount?
items.answer:
• As of now, there is no way to do this directly in the Amazon/KDP system. However, you may purchase Author Copies via KDP in quantities up to 999, and you may resell these yourself.
  • IngramSpark does not offer a gift card option Гўв‚¬вЂњ but you can purchase copies at your cost at any time.

items.question: How do I change my book price?
items.answer:
Kindle eBook and Paperback
  • Login to your account at https://kdp.amazon.com
  • Everything you need to know about changing your KDP prices can be found here, in the Help section
IngramSpark
  • Login to your account at https://www.ingramspark.com
  • From the dashboard, click your book title
  • Scroll down to Market Pricing and click the edit button
  • In the Effective Date dropdown, select the date you would like the price change to go into effect
  • Change the prices, discounts, and returnable options as you like. Price changes are scheduled and effective the first day of each month. (Changes should be submitted at least a week before the scheduled update.)
  • Click Submit

items.question: How do I change my book category?
items.answer:
Kindle eBook and Paperback
  • Login to your account at https://kdp.amazon.com
  • Everything you need to know about changing your KDP categories can be found here, in the Help section
  • Note: If you want to add your book to a category that is not available for selection, you can login to your account at Author Central and contact support with a request for your book to be added to that category.
IngramSpark
  • Login to your account at https://www.ingramspark.com
  • From the dashboard, click your book title
  • Scroll down to Categorize Your Book and click the edit button
  • Change the Subjects as you like
  • Scroll down if necessary to click Save

items.question: How do I update my Author Page, Bio, or Photo?
items.answer:
Login to your account at https://authorcentral.amazon.com
  • To update your bio:
    • Everything you need to know is here, in the Help section
  • To update your photos:
    • Everything you need to know is here, in the Help section

items.question: What are all these files you sent me for?
items.answer:
• The final publishing and source files for your book are your property, and itГўв‚¬в„ўs important to keep them in case you need to update your book in the future.
  • The final publishing files are your book covers and interiors for the different editions of your book. These are the files ending in PDF, JPG, MOBI, etc., and arenГўв‚¬в„ўt intended to be edited.
  • The source files are the editable files used to create your publishing files. These are the files ending PSD, INDD, AI, etc.

items.question: What if I need to make changes to my files?
items.answer: • If you ever need to make edits to your book files, please contact your PM to ensure the edits are made properly.

items.question: How do I view my sales and royalties information?
items.answer:
In Kindle Direct Publishing:
  • Login in to your KDP Dashboard (not Author Central)
  • Click Reports in the top menu bar
  • The default view of this chart is all marketplaces and both Kindle and Print Гўв‚¬вЂњ shown as a bar chart.
  • You can select the drop down menus to filter the information
  • If you scroll down on this main page, you will see the Royalties owed in dollars
IngramSpark:
  • Login to your account https://www.ingramspark.com
  • Click Reports in the top menu bar
  • Date range Гўв‚¬вЂњ Select a date range
  • Operating unit Гўв‚¬вЂњ Select a region Гўв‚¬вЂњ usually LS US
  • Currency Гўв‚¬вЂњ Select a currency Гўв‚¬вЂњ usually US Dollar
  • Compensation type Гўв‚¬вЂњ Select only POD
  • Optional Filter Гўв‚¬вЂњ no need to add anything here unless you have more than one title
  • Select your delivery option Гўв‚¬вЂњ on screen or email
  • Click Submit

items.question: What if I have other questions about my KDP or IngramSpark account or paperback royalty payments not covered here? Who do I call?
items.answer:
• You can always email your Publishing Manager directly and ask for help.
  • If you have problems with your KDP account or Amazon in general:
    • Login to your account at https://authorcentral.amazon.com
    • Click Help
    • Click Contact Us
    • Make a selection from the menu to define the issue and choose email or phone to specify how youГўв‚¬в„ўd like customer support to contact you
  • If you have problems with your IngramSpark account, author copies, or general printing issues:
    • Login to your account at https://www.ingramspark.com
    • In the header, click Contact Us
    • Use the listed phone numbers or emails to contact support

items.question: Can I get my book into bookstores or retailers?
items.answer:
The distribution channels we use make your book available for almost any bookstore and retailers to order if someone walks into the store and requests it. Of course, thatГўв‚¬в„ўs not the same thing as having the bookstore stock your book on the shelves.

A book sitting on a shelf doesnГўв‚¬в„ўt sell in most cases. In terms of buyer-behavior, most people who shop at Barnes & Noble also shop on Amazon, so if they donГўв‚¬в„ўt see a book at Barnes & Noble theyГўв‚¬в„ўll just go on Amazon. In other words, lack of bookstore distribution in and of itself doesnГўв‚¬в„ўt necessarily lose you very many sales.

The reality is, for almost all authors, being in a bookstore is purely about ego, and not about actual ROI. We donГўв‚¬в„ўt deny that going into a bookstore and seeing your book on the shelf is nice, but having a book in a bookstore does almost nothing in terms of sales or awareness.

Getting a book onto store shelves is very difficult. This is because bookstores carry very few books and reserve their limited space for established authors or books from publishers who are paying for shelf space.

Our advice: write a book that is so good it demands attention from booksellers so they ask you if they can sell your book.

items.question: What if I have great news about my book?
items.answer: Let us know! WeГўв‚¬в„ўd love to hear it!

items.question: What metrics do you use to determine if the Publicity Service is successful?
items.answer: We evaluate the number of opportunities presented, number of opportunities responded to, number of quotes published, the Domain Authority of each publication, the unique monthly views of each publication, and the relevance of each publicationГўв‚¬в„ўs audience.

items.question: What kind of results can I expect to see?
items.answer:
On average authors receive 4-8 opportunities each month and see 2-3 of those pieces going live.

Please note that we canГўв‚¬в„ўt guarantee placements or the timing of opportunities going live.

items.question: How much input do I have on the strategy and messaging?
items.answer:
We welcome all feedback!

But from doing this type of work for a long time we know volume of quality coverage is more important than getting in one Гўв‚¬Е“idealГўв‚¬Вќ publication at the beginning of a publicity campaign. We want to get you as much coverage as possible in the beginning of our engagement so that you are vetted and trustworthy in the eyes of bigger publications and journalists. This increases your chances of being covered by higher tier publications.

items.question: Why should I do Publicity with you?
items.answer:
We have worked with hundreds of authors looking to increase their public profile, increase books sales, drive traffic to certain websites, increase speaker bookings and promote their businesses.

Our partners have also worked with global brands like Virgin Atlantic, Google, Red Bull, Dropbox, Ebay, and more.

items.question: What can I earn?
items.answer:
• $12,000 for each Readora Elite Ghostwriting Author
  • $4,000 for each Readora Professional Author
  • $3,000 for each Guided Author
  • $1,000 for each Publishing Author

items.question: How do I succeed in the Ambassador program?
items.answer: Our most successful Ambassadors have gotten creative, mentioning us in speeches, reaching out to their email lists, and coming up with other ideas to get us in front of their audiences. HereГўв‚¬в„ўs a running list of our favorite ideas. As Cameron Herold would say, feel free to use these ideas for Гўв‚¬Е“R&DГўв‚¬Вќ (rip-off and duplicate).

items.question: How should I talk about Readora to potential authors?
items.answer:
• What we do: Readora helps entrepreneurs, executives and high-level professionals write, publish, and market their non-fiction books.
  • Why we do it:
    • Readora believes everyone on earth has a story to tell.
    • We want to help people share their unique knowledge and leave a legacy of impact.
    • We believe everyone should be able to write and publish their book, while maintaining ownership and creative controlГўв‚¬вЂќand still ensuring the highest quality and impact for readers.
  • How we do it: We surround authors with a professional publishing team to help them position, write, edit, design, publish, and market their book, getting it out of their head and out into the world.
  • Who weГўв‚¬в„ўve worked with: WeГўв‚¬в„ўve worked with over 1,700 authors to create some incredible books, including 11 New York Times or Wall Street Journal bestsellers in just the last few years.
    • David Goggins, NY Times, WSJ Bestseller & USA Today Bestseller
    • Tiffany Haddish, NYT Bestseller, Goodreads Best Books of 2018
    • Nobel Peace Prize Committee
    • Todd Herman, WSJ Bestseller
    • Joey Coleman, WSJ Bestseller

items.question: When I tell people about Readora, some people tell me that itГўв‚¬в„ўs too expensive.
items.answer: Our service is geared towards individuals who prioritize their time over their budget. Like most things, there is a huge range in the price and quality of writing and publishing services Гўв‚¬вЂќ you can find writers on Craigslist that are $500 for a book, and premium ghostwriters as expensive as $150k. Having a team of professionals handling every aspect of a book is inherently expensive because of the costs of such great talent. For the quality we offer, we believe our prices are extremely low. But, because it's a lot of money, it is most beneficial for individuals who can see a clear return on investment from publishing their book, such as business owners and speakers. For people with more time than money who would like a lower cost option, they are welcome to follow our process by downloading our book, The Readora Method, for free and following the steps on their own time.

items.question: When will I receive my referral commission?
items.answer:
YouГўв‚¬в„ўll receive your referral commission in three installments as the author you referred reaches major milestones in their project:
  • First installment (10%) Гўв‚¬вЂќ after the initial milestone (such as manuscript interviews, coaching, or cover stage depending on the service).
  • Second installment (40%) Гўв‚¬вЂќ at the approximate halfway milestone of the project.
  • Third installment (50%) Гўв‚¬вЂќ upon project completion.
Commissions are processed at the end of each month, and youГўв‚¬в„ўll receive an email from us as each payment becomes available. All payments are handled securely through our trusted payment platform, Plooto.

items.question: Can you help me with [insert weird idea]?
items.answer: Almost always, yes. Unless itГўв‚¬в„ўs illegal, immoral, or otherwise problematic, we are always happy to test out new ideas and work with our Ambassadors to make referrals as fun and easy as possible. WeГўв‚¬в„ўve helped Ambassadors write speeches, host webinars, create landing pages, put together custom offers, and just about everything in between. It canГўв‚¬в„ўt hurt to ask :)

items.question: What if I have other ideas about how to promote Readora?
items.answer: ThatГўв‚¬в„ўs great! Although our goal is to avoiding putting too much work on our Ambassadors, we are always open to creative ideas. WeГўв‚¬в„ўve had Ambassadors give speeches, run social media campaigns, and do all sorts of other creative marketing to help refer more authors.

items.question: Can I be an ambassador if I'm not an author that worked previously with Readora?
items.answer: Absolutely! As long as you know people who might make great potential authors, we want to hear from you.

items.question: What is the difference between a non-fiction or knowledge share book and a memoir?
items.answer: A non-fiction knowledge share book is any non-fiction book you're writing primarily to help people solove a problem or create a transformation. It can be a business, personal development/self-help, or a how-to book. A memoir tells your story to move or entertain the reader.

items.question: Should you have a lawyer look at your book before publishing if you're worried about being sued?
items.answer: Yes. But you don't have to worry about this until you're ready to publish. For now, just write your draft.

items.question: How will I know if my book is original?
items.answer: The truth is, nothing is completely 'original' Гўв‚¬вЂќ everything is based off something else. What is original is your interpretation of what you know and how you speak to your audience.

items.question: What type of tools do you recommend to assist in writing a book?
items.answer: We find that people get far too distracted with tools when it comes to writing. Don't get caught up on this Гўв‚¬вЂќ a simple Word processor or Google docs will do if you're not already using something else.

items.question: How do I know if I should write a book?
items.answer: If you have unique knowledge about something you want to share, feel like you have a story in you that you need to tell, or want to get more clients and improve your career, then you should write a book.

items.question: Should I write a book if I'm not sure I can sell a lot of copies?
items.answer: Absolutely. In fact, the odds are you won't sell many copies. There are many other reasons to write a book. Like sharing your knowledge, impacting others, boosting your career, and becoming a thought leader in your field.

items.question: Can writing a book help boost my career?
items.answer: Absolutely. We've seen hundreds of authors get more paid speaking opportunities, more clients, and more revenue after their book boosts their status and credibility in their field.

items.question: How can I tell if my target audience is niche enough, or too broad?
items.answer: First, identify the niche. Then, ask yourself: who are the dominant players in this field/market/subject? If you can't name anyone, it's not saturated. If you can name one person, there's room for a second. If you can name three, you probably shouldn't go into it. This is a general rule, though, and can be broken.

items.question: How does it work getting illustrations into my book?
items.answer: You hire an illustrator who has experience in the type of illustrations you want. Preferably, choose an illustrator with book experience.

items.question: How do I decide on a book cover? Who can help me with the design?
items.answer: Remember this: Your book will be judged by it's cover. We recommend that you hire a book cover designer and be ready to share who your target reader is, have a few book covers that you like as samples, talk about what you want your cover to feel like, discuss the signals you are trying to send to your audience, and if you can, discuss the emotions you want your reader to feel when they see he cover.

items.question: What are the different types of non-fiction books?
items.answer: There are Memoirs and Knowledge Share books. Memoirs which consist of: personal story, autobiography, and family legacy. Knowledge share books consist of: how-to, business, personal development, and self-help books.

items.question: What are the most common stumbling blocks people encounter when trying to write their first book?
items.answer: It's normal to hit stumbling blocks Гўв‚¬вЂќ everyone does. The most common are different manifestations of fear (my book won't be good enough, I don't have it in me), not using a process that works, and not being held accountable.

items.question: Is there an approximate length a memoir should be?
items.answer: Length doesnГўв‚¬в„ўt matter for memoirГўв‚¬вЂќwhat matters is getting your truth out in the way you need to.

items.question: If my book is too niche, do I risk losing a larger audience?
items.answer: Actually, no. Although it may sound counterintuitive, 'the riches are in the niches.' You don't want your book to be for everyone. Instead, you want it to be for a specific group of people. The best book positioning Гўв‚¬вЂќ like the best branding Гўв‚¬вЂќ not only attracts the right people, it repels the wrong people.

items.question: How many chapters is a good rule of thumb for a non-fiction book?
items.answer: There are no Гўв‚¬Е“rulesГўв‚¬Вќ for how many words you should have for book chapters.

items.question: What are the biggest mistakes people make when choosing their book audience?
items.answer:
1. They make their audience broad, instead of niche. A niche audience is narrow and deep while a broad audience is wide and shallow. Same rules apply when digging for oil.
  2. They don't know why their audience will care. Remember, readers don't care about your book but what your book will do for them. If you don't understand what it is they care about, they won't care.

items.question: How can you tell when a niche is too saturated?
items.answer: First, identify the niche. Then, ask yourself: who are the dominant players in this field/market/subject? If you can't name anyone, it's not saturated. If you can name one person, there's room for a second. If you can name three, you probably shouldn't go into it.

items.question: What do I do if I'm worried my memoir will upset my family?
items.answer: Write your first draft while not thinking or worry about it. Then, when it's time to edit, go back and edit it in a way that feels good to you. However, remember that writing a memoir is about writing your truth. Sometimes, the truth may hurt.

items.question: Do traditional publishers do marketing for their authors?
items.answer: No. Whether you publish with a traditional publisher or self publish, you will be responsible for your own marketing efforts.

items.question: Is it better to have a writing schedule or just write when I'm feeling inspired?
items.answer: We suggest sitting down to write at least 250 words per day. You can always write more, or less, but we've found setting an acheiveable daily goal is the best way to stick to writing every day.

items.question: Where should I write my book? Is there a place that is the most conducive to writing?
items.answer: Wherever you get writing done. Consider ambient noise, temperature, view, comfort, and isolation. A universal Гўв‚¬Е“correctГўв‚¬Вќ place to write doesnГўв‚¬в„ўt exist. If you write well in coffee shops, do that. If you write well at a desk in your basement, do that. Wherever you are most creative, most functional, and most confident, write there.

items.question: What book writing software do I use to write?
items.answer: It doesn't matter what book writing software you use. Just don't get fancy. Use what you know and what is easiest.

items.question: What if I want to get my book translated?
items.answer: Sell your translation rights to a foreign publisher who will translate and release the book in their own local market.

items.question: Who is it for?
items.answer: This book writing course is designed for people who want to write a non-fiction book, primarily in the broad genres of business, personal development, self-help, or related areas.

items.question: What should you expect?
items.answer:
You should expect the very best instruction in the world on how to position, structure, outline, write and edit a non-fiction book. 

Everything you need to know on how to write a book in contained in this course, and explained in a detailed, step-by-step way.  

This is the exact process that we use with our clients who pay us between $10k and $40k to help them write and publish their books. The only difference is that they are paying for our personalized attention, time and execution. The knowledge and information is exactly the same.

items.question: How can this be totally free? What is the catch?
items.answer: We believe that everyone on earth should write and publish a book. Giving this book publishing course away for free is our way of proving weГўв‚¬в„ўre serious about achieving that goal. We could easily sell this course for $2,000 or more. There are entire companies that basically do nothing but sell courses like this (and they are inferior to this one). WeГўв‚¬в„ўve made a conscious decision to forgo all the revenue we could get from selling this information, because we feel itГўв‚¬в„ўs far more important to have it easily accessible. ThatГўв‚¬в„ўs why we donГўв‚¬в„ўt have any gate on it at all. WeГўв‚¬в„ўre the premier book writing and publishing services company on earth with a mission to help everyone on earth write and publish their book, but if we only provided services, that wouldnГўв‚¬в„ўt totally fulfill our mission. We love books (and serving people) more than we like money, and we and believe books are crucial to civilization, so we are willing to stand for making it possible for anyone to write and publish their bookГўв‚¬вЂќwhile still ensuring they are high quality and impactful for readers. We believe everyoneГўв‚¬вЂќespecially the people who canГўв‚¬в„ўt afford our servicesГўв‚¬вЂќshould be able to have access to the exact same information as our clients, so their books can be just as professional as the people who can pay for our services. We do this because we believe everyone on earth should write their book, publish their book, own their book, and have the best book possible--so their book can make the biggest possible impact. This is how we are going to make that happen.

items.question: How do I determine if someone will be a good fit?
items.answer:
To determine if someone would be a good fit for our professional services, ask yourself these questions:
  1. Are they an expert in their field, as an entrepreneur, executive, or thought leader?
  2. Can people benefit from what they teach?
  3. Would publishing their book help grow their business, or start a new one?
  4. Would they be able to invest $20k+ for expert guidance to write and publish their book?

items.question: How many new clients do you take on each month?
items.answer:
We sign 10 new projects each month. There are sometimes exceptions to this rule, but this limited number of books allows us to provide the best service to our authors.

This is typically on a first-come, first-serve basis.

items.question: Does Readora work with anyone?
items.answer:
No, we cannot just work with anyone. We typically reject about 30% of authors who want to work with us. There are two criteria we use to decide which authors weГўв‚¬в„ўll work with:
  1. Does the author know the subject matter of their book deeply enough to write a book about it? Our process works very well, but only if the author actually knows what theyГўв‚¬в„ўre talking about.
  2. Does the author have a clear understanding of how books work and a reasonable expectation for ROI? This means there needs to be a purpose behind writing the book.
ThatГўв‚¬в„ўs why the first step in our process is a qualification process. If the result youГўв‚¬в„ўre hoping for is unrealistic or impossible, we canГўв‚¬в„ўt work with you, because thereГўв‚¬в„ўs no way for us to win. Even if we do a great job and we nail the book, you might still be upset because you didnГўв‚¬в„ўt reach a goal that was impossible to begin with.

items.question: Is there anyone who should not work with Readora?
items.answer:
Yes, definitely. Here is a partial list of the types of people who are not a good fit for our process:
  1. Someone who doesn't know their topic well. If you want a book on sales, for example, but donГўв‚¬в„ўt know anything about sales, then we canГўв‚¬в„ўt help you.
  2. Someone who isn't sure how a book can help them.
  3. Someone who has no idea at all why they want to write a book, what it will say, or what goals theyГўв‚¬в„ўll achieve by publishing it. Our process requires an investmentГўв‚¬вЂќof both your money and your effortГўв‚¬вЂќso you should have an idea of why you are doing it.

items.question: Can you work in other languages?
items.answer: No, not right now.

items.question: What if I'm not sure I have enough to say for a book?
items.answer:
ThatГўв‚¬в„ўs precisely why you should talk to us before you startГўв‚¬вЂќweГўв‚¬в„ўll help you to make sure your idea is good enough for a book.

Our process does not work unless you actually have a bookГўв‚¬в„ўs worth of ideas in your head to start. So the very first step in our processГўв‚¬вЂќbefore we startГўв‚¬вЂќis ensuring that you do, in fact, have a book in you.

Not everyone has a book in them, and weГўв‚¬в„ўre happy to tell you that, if thatГўв‚¬в„ўs the case. We only want to work with people who have a good book idea that will be interesting to readers and bring the author a clear ROI.

items.question: What if I have more than one book in me?
items.answer:
WeГўв‚¬в„ўve found this to be the case with most authorsГўв‚¬вЂќthey actually have more than one book in them. Someone who has spent a lot of time gaining deep expertise in a field typically has a lot of knowledge that would be more interesting to people than they realize. For the majority of our Authors, the question is not whether they have a book in them, itГўв‚¬в„ўs how many, and which one should they publish first.

Our process is designed to help you figure out which book you should do first, and why. We begin by helping you to understand exactly why youГўв‚¬в„ўre writing a book, what results you want, which of your ideas will make the best book for you, and how to make it happen.

And noteГўв‚¬вЂќwe do this before you sign with us and get started. Before you commit to spending a dollar, we want to make sure weГўв‚¬в„ўre both a great fit for each other.

items.question: Is Readora Book School free?
items.answer: Yes. And always will be.

items.question: What if I already know my book idea?
items.answer: GreatГўв‚¬вЂќCompass will help pressure-test and strengthen your vision to ensure it resonates with your audience.

items.question: What if I decide not to move forward with Readora afterward?
items.answer: You still keep your deliverables and own everythingГўв‚¬вЂќyou can self-publish or pitch the book elsewhere.

items.question: Who qualifies to be an ambassador?
items.answer:
Our Ambassador Program is open to both authors and non-authors who are passionate about sharing the impact of publishing with Readora. While many of our Ambassadors are authors who have gone through the process themselves, we also partner with professionals and organizations who regularly support thought leaders and business owners. Examples include:
  • Strategic coaches and business advisors
  • Chambers of Commerce and professional associations
  • Marketing agencies and publicists
  • Literary agents and book consultants
If you have a network of individuals who could benefit from publishing their book, or if youГўв‚¬в„ўre in a position to guide leaders toward amplifying their voices, you may be a great fit for our program. Our goal is to create meaningful, mutually beneficial partnerships with those who are excited to champion authorship and thought leadership.

items.question: How is payment handled?
items.answer:
Rather than waiting until the project is fully complete, youГўв‚¬в„ўll receive your referral commission in three installments as the author you referred reaches major milestones in their project:
  • First installment (10%) Гўв‚¬вЂќ after the initial milestone (such as manuscript interviews, coaching, or cover stage depending on the service).
  • Second installment (40%) Гўв‚¬вЂќ at the approximate halfway milestone of the project.
  • Third installment (50%) Гўв‚¬вЂќ upon project completion.
This structure ensures that Ambassadors are rewarded promptly for successful referrals, while also aligning payments with the authorГўв‚¬в„ўs progress. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs a straightforward, transparent process designed to make your partnership with us both rewarding and reliable. Commissions are processed at the end of each month, and youГўв‚¬в„ўll receive an email from us as each payment becomes available. All payments are handled securely through our trusted payment platform, Plooto.

items.question: WhatГўв‚¬в„ўs the difference between Readora Signature and Readora Professional?
items.answer: Readora Signature includes everything in Readora Professional, plus expanded collaboration, additional writing and revision calls, and a flexible budget for research, design, or custom creative elements. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs designed for complex or high-stakes projects that require more time, creativity, and precision.

items.question: Who will I work with on my book?
items.answer: YouГўв‚¬в„ўll be paired with one of our most experienced ReadorasГўв‚¬вЂќwriters who have mastered the art of turning complex ideas into compelling books. YouГўв‚¬в„ўll also have a full production team behind you, including editors, designers, and publishing experts dedicated to your project.

items.question: What types of authors is Signature best for?
items.answer: Signature is ideal for leaders, experts, and professionals with a big vision for their bookГўв‚¬вЂќwhether itГўв‚¬в„ўs a deeply personal memoir, a technical or research-driven work, or a brand-defining project that requires creative depth and polish.

items.question: Can I add my co-author or contributors?
items.answer: Yes. Signature includes multiple authors or contributors at no additional cost (unless youГўв‚¬в„ўve assembled an entire team). WeГўв‚¬в„ўll coordinate interviews and collaboration to ensure every voice is captured seamlessly.

items.question: What if I do want to move forward with Readora afterward?
items.answer: PerfectГўв‚¬вЂќyour $5,000 Compass payment will be applied if you choose to move forward with Readora Professional, Readora Elite, or Guided Author.

items.question: Do I have to write my book right after?
items.answer: Not at all. Many Compass clients use the clarity to wait, refine, or confidently transition into Readora production later.

items.question: Can I refer more than one person?
items.answer: Absolutely! ThereГўв‚¬в„ўs no limit to the number of people you can refer through the Ambassador Program. Many of our Ambassadors find that once they share their experience and knowledge, multiple people in their network are interested in learning more. Each qualified referral is eligible for Ambassador payments, making it possible to create ongoing rewards while helping more authors bring their books to life.

items.question: Do you offer audiobook creation and distribution?
items.answer:
Yes! We have an in-house audiobook department and access to a network of thousands of narrators. This ensures the highest quality for your audiobook and the best narrator possible.

If you prefer to narrate your audiobook, weГўв‚¬в„ўll set you up at one of our partner studios to ensure you are expertly coached during your narration.

Distribution is handled through our friends at AuthorГўв‚¬в„ўs Republic and will place your audiobook on all the major platforms, including Amazon, iTunes, Spotify, and more.

items.question: Can I hire someone to narrate my audiobook for me?
items.answer: Absolutely. WeГўв‚¬в„ўll utilize our network of 1,000+ professional narrators to find the perfect voice for you based on your book and preferences. This will ensure that the audiobook is produced with the highest audio quality and in compliance with the specifications and standards that are demanded by the major audiobook platforms.

items.question: How can I make an audiobook after IГўв‚¬в„ўve written my book?
items.answer:
You have a few options:
  • You can source a narrator and production team yourself and hire them to produce your audiobook.
  • You can find a local studio and narrate your own audiobook and find an audio engineer to help you with post-production.
  • You can hire us to record, produce and distribute your audiobook, with either yourself or a professional narrator handling the narration.
Readora Media will be your partner in assisting you through the entire audiobook process from talent selection, recording, editing, producing, and distribution. You can rest assured that you will have a high quality audiobook that your listeners will enjoy.

items.question: How do I estimate how long my audiobook will be?
items.answer: Dividing the word count by 9,000 will give you an estimate on the final running time of the book. For example, a typical 50,000 word manuscript (200 book pages) will be roughly 5.5 hours of finished audio.

items.question: How long does it take to record an audiobook?
items.answer: A professional narrator will record close to a 2:1 ratio. This means that for every 2 hours they spend recording, 1 hour of audio will be used. Authors or newer narrators typically record at a 3:1 ratio which extends the recording time.

items.question: IГўв‚¬в„ўm going to narrate my audiobook. How do I prepare?
items.answer:
Long-form narration can be a strenuous task. To prepare, spend as much time as possible reading your manuscript out loud, restarting sentences when you make a mistake.

You can also use annotation software to give yourself subtle reminders to pause or breathe, and highlight words youГўв‚¬в„ўre having trouble with.

While many Authors love the idea of using their own voice to record their book, in most cases, we highly recommend working with one of our professional narrators as narrating an audiobook is a specialized skill. It is not just reading a book out loud. It is offering a captivating performance that holds your listeners' attention throughout.

Whether you would like to narrate the audiobook yourself, or if you would like to hire one of our 1,000+ professional narrators, Readora Media will be your partner through the entire audiobook process from talent selection to distribution.

items.question: How long does it take to fully produce an audiobook?
items.answer: Readora Media can deliver a retail-ready audiobook within 2-3 months from signing your agreement.

items.question: If I do Readora Elite Ghostwriting, am I the author of the book? Is it in my voice?
items.answer: Yes, youГўв‚¬в„ўre the sole author of your book, and itГўв‚¬в„ўs entirely in your voice. You are the only one contributing the ideas, the knowledge, and the content to the book, (and with the Guided Author Program, you actually write it out as well). All we do is help you with the parts of book writing that you arenГўв‚¬в„ўt an expert at; positioning, structuring, etc. WeГўв‚¬в„ўre able to do this because the book is literally created entirely from interviews with youГўв‚¬вЂќthere is no other voice it could be but yours.

items.question: How do you select my Ghostwriter? Can I choose them?
items.answer:
No, authors do not choose their own Ghostwriter. That is why you are paying usГўв‚¬вЂќto do that.

To begin with, we thoroughly vet and test all our Ghostwriters. ItГўв‚¬в„ўs easier to get into Harvard (6% acceptance rate) than it is to work with us as a Readora (<1% acceptance rate). Not only that, but our Ghostwriters have spent at least a decade (usually longer) in writing and publishing, and many of them are highly decoratedГўв‚¬вЂќPulitzer Prize winners, Emmy nominees and winners, and National Magazine Award winners.

We have an internal Гўв‚¬Е“ask systemГўв‚¬Вќ that we use to pair up the right Ghostwriter and Author (you). HereГўв‚¬в„ўs how it works:

We get to know you, your book, and how you work. Then we create a Гўв‚¬Е“Project ScopeГўв‚¬Вќ for your book, and let all our Ghostwriters see it. They will read it, and the ones who are interested in working on your book ask to be assigned.

They answer two questions: 1. Why are you excited to work on this book, and 2. What do you bring to the book that might help the author?

We then pick the one we think will be the best matchГўв‚¬вЂќ95% of the time, this process works great.

But yes, in rare cases, the Author and Ghostwriter are not a fit. If after working together, they feel uncomfortable or unwilling to move forward with the project, weГўв‚¬в„ўre happy to find a replacement Ghostwriter.

items.question: Who will I be working with during this process?
items.answer:
Your publishing team, which will be led by your Publishing Manager. They are your main point of contact and are in charge of ensuring you get a great book.

Most of the writing will be done by your Readora, who is a professional writer and editor with at least a decade of experience working on books.

You will conceptualize your book cover with the help of our Creative Director, who has done dozens of bestselling book covers. The cover will then be designed by the director or someone on her team, and you will talk to our Art Director about feedback and revisions.

Your marketing efforts will be led by our book launch and PR team to ensure your book reaches its audience and achieves your goals as a newly published Author.

items.question: WhatГўв‚¬в„ўs included in the Guided Author program?
items.answer: You get everything you need to confidently write, publish, and market your book. Literally everything. The entire program is described in detail here.

items.question: Is editing included? How does the editing work?
items.answer: Yes, editing is included. Once you finish the rough draft of your manuscript, then you hand it to us for a complete structural edit. Your manuscript will be fully edited by a member of our Executive Editing team, someone who has at least 15 years experience in book editing. As to how we edit, we tell our authors this: Гўв‚¬Е“We will hurt your feelings before we let you publish a bad book.Гўв‚¬Вќ Our first goal in this edit is to ensure your book is not bad. If it is, we will tell you where itГўв‚¬в„ўs bad and how to fix it. Once we know itГўв‚¬в„ўs not bad (and they are rarely bad), then the goal is to help you take it from good to great. We will push you to write the best book possible, because our name is on the book as well as yours.

items.question: Is publishing included? How does publishing work?
items.answer: Yes, everything necessary to publish your book is included: interior layout, cover design, distribution, everything. In fact, the publishing process is the same as what we use with our Readora Professional authors.

items.question: Do I have to take 15 months, or can I go faster?
items.answer:
You get to pay for the Guided Author service over 15 months, but you can publish in as little as 9 months. The difference depends entirely on how long it takes you to write and edit your manuscript.

The only catch is that for us to fully publish your book, you must be paid in full.
